name: Double-Berry Shortcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. In a medium bowl, combine flour and 1/4 cup sugar; add 1 1/3 cups heavy cream. With a fork, stir until the dough begins to form a soft ball. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface; knead gently 4 times. Pat into a 10x3 inch strip; cut crosswise into 6 lengths. Place on a baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool on a wire rack.
2. In a medium saucepan, mash strawberries with a potato masher or fork. Reserve 2 tablespoons sugar; add the remaining to mashed strawberries. Bring to a boil and cook 1 minute. Pour into a bowl; chill thoroughly.
3. Slice the remaining strawberries and add them to the cooled mashed berries. Add blueberries; mix.
4. In a small bowl of electric mixer, whip the remaining cream with reserved sugar until soft peaks form.
5. To serve: Split shortcakes horizontally, fill with berries and whipped cream. Replace tops.
